mission.title: alertmanager: Microsoft deprecating Teams webhooks, new "Workflows" uses incompatible schema
mission.description: Microsoft deprecating Teams webhooks, new "Workflows" uses incompatible schema. This issue affects 44+ users.

steps.title: Identify alertmanager upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your alertmanager deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager -n alertmanager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review alertmanager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager
```
Update: For a (seemingly functional) workaround see my comment https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/issues/3920#issuecomment-2219950227 below.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Microsoft deprecating Teams webhooks, new "Workflows" uses…
Confirm Microsoft deprecating Teams webhooks, new… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager -n alertmanager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
tl;dr
Plop JSON in the text field and parse it from PowerAutomate.

I am working on a hacky solution atm.
We can use Power automation functions to parse JSON.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
